text: Use usethis::use_standalone("WangLabCSU/rpkgkit", "<name>") import: args_to_func.R: matching arguments function calls: caller_cli.R: show cli function called colorful_cli.R: Easier color calling one cli function match_arg.R: partial matching arguments function calls, like match.arg, rlang::arg_match ts_cli.R: timestamp cli function
code: f1 <- function(a, b) a + b f2 <- function(x, y, ...) x * y f3 <- function(p, q) p - q  args <- list(a = 1, b = 2)  # Strict matching (default): returns f1 only match_func_to_args(args, f1, f2, f3) args <- list(a = 1, b = 2, x = 3, y = 4) foo <- function(x , y = 1){x + y} filter_args_for_func(args, foo) # Keeps arguments for foo # $x # [1] 3  # $y # [1] 4 decorated <- add_caller_to_cli(cli::cli_alert_info) foo1 <- \() {   print("I' m in foo1")   decorated("<- where is this called from?") } foo2 <- \() {   print("I' m in foo2")   foo1() } bar <- function() {   print("I' m in bar")   foo2() } bar() # [1] "I' m in bar" # [1] "I' m in foo2" # [1] "I' m in foo1" # ℹ [foo1()]: <- where is this called from? color_cli <- create_colorful_cli_env() color_cli$cli_alert_danger("{.red This is a red message}") color_cli$cli_alert_info("{.blue This is a blue message}") color_cli$cli_alert_info("{.orange This is an orange message}") # cyan, green, magenta, yellow, purple, etc  color_cli2 <- create_colorful_cli_env(cli_theme = generate_color_theme()) # more color but slower color_cli2$cli_alert_success(   "{.violetred3 R}{.orange a}{.yellow i}{.green n}{.cyan b}{.blue o}{.purple w}" ) ts_cli <- create_ts_cli_env() ts_cli$cli_alert_info("Hello, world!") # ℹ [2026/05/30 22:45:42] Hello, world!

title: Make function calls explicit — add_double_colons
text: function behaves differently depending context. package development: current context package development, make function calls explicit using currently attached packages (.e. ones attached calls library()). Package development: detects current context package development make function calls explicit using packages 'Imports' field package DESCRIPTION. package developed imports packages entirety (.e Import pkg appears NAMESPACE file), calls functions packages left unchanged. See current_packages() information.

title: Make function calls explicit — add_double_colons
code: code <- "   cars <- as_tibble(mtcars)   cars %>%     filter(mpg > 20) %>%     summarise(across(everything(), n_distinct)) "  # Code will be transformed to use the double-colon operator, but notice # that `n_distinct` is not transformed as it is not followed by `()` cat(add_double_colons(code, "dplyr")) #>  #>   cars <- dplyr::as_tibble(mtcars) #>   cars %>% #>     dplyr::filter(mpg > 20) %>% #>     dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), n_distinct))  # You can specify functions that shouldn't be transformed: cat(add_double_colons(code, "dplyr", ignore_functions = "across")) #>  #>   cars <- dplyr::as_tibble(mtcars) #>   cars %>% #>     dplyr::filter(mpg > 20) %>% #>     dplyr::summarise(across(dplyr::everything(), n_distinct))  # Beware namespace conflicts! The following are not the same, mimicking # the effects of reordering calls to `library()`: cat(add_double_colons(code, c("dplyr", "stats"))) #>  #>   cars <- dplyr::as_tibble(mtcars) #>   cars %>% #>     dplyr::filter(mpg > 20) %>% #>     dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), n_distinct))  cat(add_double_colons(code, c("stats", "dplyr"))) #>  #>   cars <- dplyr::as_tibble(mtcars) #>   cars %>% #>     stats::filter(mpg > 20) %>% #>     dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::everything(), n_distinct))

what: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
title: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
text: Functions help manage NEWS.md file R packages according CRAN guidelines. Includes functions add new entries check format. Validates NEWS.md file CRAN guidelines common best practices. Reports issues found provides suggestions fixes. Reads displays NEWS.md file content optional filtering. Adds new entry NEWS.md file following CRAN guidelines. Can create new version section needed add existing one.

title: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
code: news_md_check(path = NULL, strict = FALSE, verbose = TRUE)  news_md_show(path = NULL, version = NULL, max_versions = NULL)  news_md_add_entry(   entry,   version = NULL,   category = "NEW FEATURES",   contributor = NULL,   path = NULL,   date = NULL,   open_section = TRUE )

title: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
text: path Path package root. NULL (default), uses current working directory. strict TRUE, treats warnings errors. Default FALSE. verbose TRUE, prints detailed information checks performed. version Package version number. NULL, uses version DESCRIPTION. max_versions Maximum number versions display. NULL shows . entry Text news entry (without leading "* "). category Category change (e.g., "NEW FEATURES", "BUG FIXES", "MINOR IMPROVEMENTS", "DOCUMENTATION", "DEPRECATED", "DEFUNCT", "BREAKING CHANGES", "PERFORMANCE", "TESTING", "INTERNAL CHANGES"). Default "NEW FEATURES". contributor GitHub username name attribution (optional). date Date release. NULL, uses today's date (YYYY-MM-DD format). open_section TRUE version section exists open (content ), creates new section. FALSE, adds existing section.

title: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
text: list components: valid Logical indicating NEWS.md passes checks. errors Character vector error messages. warnings Character vector warning messages. suggestions Character vector improvement suggestions. Invisibly returns content character vector. Invisibly returns path NEWS.md file.

title: Manage NEWS.md file for R packages — news_md
text: NEWS.md format follows CRAN recommendations: Version headers use # followed "package version (date)" Major changes use ## category names (e.g., "NEW FEATURES", "BUG FIXES") Individual items use bullet points starting * Contributor acknowledgments use (@username) end items Dates YYYY-MM-DD format

what: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to auto-update R package version — use_workflow_version_update
title: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to auto-update R package version — use_workflow_version_update
text: Copies built-version_update.yml workflow template target package's .github/workflows/ directory. workflow automatically bumps R package version based commit messages, manually via workflow_dispatch specified version type. Version bump rules (commit messages, case-insensitive): major / breaking - increments major version (X.0.0) feat / feature / minor - increments minor version (x.Y.0) patch / fix / bug - increments patch version (x.y.Z) Otherwise, version bump occurs workflow_dispatch input always overrides commit message detection.
